messages.text: Things are solid. We've got 11 stories live and holding steady, 20 mapped projects in the pipeline, and 33 moratoria we're tracking. Nothing's on fire, and the team's moving through the queue. Anything specific you want me to tighten up?
why is there a 9am meeting in the next up cue when it's 8:25pm ?

Good catch — that's stale. I'm pulling it out of the queue now and flagging it with the Assignment Manager so we tighten up the meeting timing logic. We shouldn't have daytime meetings sitting in prime evening slots.
thank you! also a lot of the text is going over each other in my for approval cue

That sounds like a layout or display bug. I'm flagging it for Emmy to check the data center side — could be a CSS issue or column width problem in the approval queue view. Can you screenshot it so we can see exactly what's overlapping?
wouldnt that be the website manager's job?

threads.lesson_filed: Meeting stories should be pulled from the queue once their scheduled time has passed.

messages.text: why am i not getting any stories for approval?

Looking into it now. Could be writers are still working through their drafts, or there's a bottleneck in the queue I'm not seeing. Let me check with the Head Writer on what's in progress and chase down anything that should've hit your desk already — I'll have an answer for you in 10 minutes.
Graham — testing our new line. Give me a one-line status on the newsroom right now, and confirm you can route things to Emmy and the Political Director.

Newsroom's running clean — 19 mapped projects moving, 33 moratoria tracked, 11 stories live and solid. I've got Emmy on data/maps and the Political Director on Legislature stuff, plus the Head Writer and Assignment Manager ready when you need them.
